applies_to_stacks.launch_implication_per_token_theta: under (B) per-token-theta, launch-eligibility depends on the human's chosen theta; the pass-fraction curve is the menu (e.g. a 5%-tolerance reading passes only the cleanest few percent of sequences).
applies_to_stacks.launch_implication_ppl_only: under (C) PPL-only (the auto-scorer's actual check), BOTH stacks pass 100% (served PPL 2.3772 <= 2.42) -> launch-eligible today.
applies_to_stacks.launch_implication_strict_A: under (A) strict per-sequence token-identity, BOTH the 481.53 frontier and the land #71 tree pass only 12.5% of sequences -> NEITHER is launch-eligible; only the wirbel #199/#213/#216 compliant-kernel (batch-invariant verify) route survives strict-A.

clustering_band.note: iid Bernoulli(p=0.5608) predicts strict-A=(1-p)^512=10^-182.9 ~ 0; the EMPIRICAL strict-A is 0.1250, ~182 orders of magnitude higher, because the #114 flips CASCADE (extreme within-sequence clustering). The model-free Frechet bound caps strict-A at 1-p=0.4392 for ANY correlation structure; the empirical 0.1250 sits inside (iid 1.14e-183 ... empirical 0.1250 ... max 0.4392).

correlation_sensitivity.note: positive within-sequence flip ICC concentrates flips into fewer sequences -> strict-A RISES with clustering. But the model-free Frechet cap is 1-p=0.4392 < 0.5: even in the MOST flip-clustered world the MAJORITY (>=56.1%) of sequences still fail strict-A. The empirical 0.1250 is well below the cap. NO-GO-under-strict-A SURVIVES the full clustering sweep -> the int4-spec stack fails literal token-identity robustly.

handoff_lines.fern_185: the #192 per-reading menu from #114's 56.08%: strict-A (per-sequence zero-flip) pass = 0.1250 (16/128; the int4-spec stack fails literal token-identity for 88% of sequences — and even under the model-free maximal-flip-clustering cap 1-p=0.439 it stays < 0.5, so NO-GO-under-strict-A is robust), per-token-theta pass-fraction = the empirical CDF (theta=0 -> 0.1250, theta=1 -> 1.0), PPL-only (the auto-scorer's actual check) = 100% (served PPL 2.3772 <= 2.42); BOTH the 481.53 frontier and the land #71 tree share this exposure, so the human's A/B/C ruling determines whether EITHER is launch-eligible under strict-A — and the wirbel #199/#213/#216 compliant-kernel route is the only strict-A-survivable 500-path.
handoff_lines.issue_192: the #192 per-reading menu from #114's 56.08%: strict-A (per-sequence zero-flip) pass = 0.1250 (16/128; the int4-spec stack fails literal token-identity for 88% of sequences — and even under the model-free maximal-flip-clustering cap 1-p=0.439 it stays < 0.5, so NO-GO-under-strict-A is robust), per-token-theta pass-fraction = the empirical CDF (theta=0 -> 0.1250, theta=1 -> 1.0), PPL-only (the auto-scorer's actual check) = 100% (served PPL 2.3772 <= 2.42); BOTH the 481.53 frontier and the land #71 tree share this exposure, so the human's A/B/C ruling determines whether EITHER is launch-eligible under strict-A — and the wirbel #199/#213/#216 compliant-kernel route is the only strict-A-survivable 500-path.

reading_definitions.A_strict_per_sequence: a served greedy sequence is COMPLIANT iff ALL its output tokens match plain greedy AR (zero argmax flips over output_len). pass = fraction of the 128 served sequences with zero flips.
reading_definitions.B_per_token_theta: COMPLIANT iff the per-sequence flip fraction <= theta. pass_fraction(theta) is the empirical CDF of per-sequence flip fractions over theta in [0,1].
reading_definitions.C_ppl_only: COMPLIANT iff served PPL <= 2.42 (the auto-scorer's actual check). served PPL 2.3772 <= 2.42 -> 100%.
reading_definitions.source_granularity_note: #114 interlock_report.json banks the per-SEQUENCE split (num_identical=16, num_divergent=112) AND the per-POSITION token-ids (decode_outputs.jsonl, spec-ON + spec-OFF AR). This leg reconstructs the full per-sequence flip-fraction distribution from the per-position ids and verifies it reproduces the banked per-sequence split + aggregate bit-for-bit -> strict-A is OBSERVED, not modeled.

reading_pass_fractions.ordering_note: strict-A 0.1250 (theta=0) <= per-token-theta(theta) (monotone CDF) <= PPL-only 1.0. The three readings are nested in strictness.

reconstruction.anchor_note: per-position reconstruction reproduces #114 banked split BIT-FOR-BIT: identical 16/128 (banked 16), divergent 112 (banked 112), token_div_frac 0.5607757568359375 (banked 0.5607757568359375).

synthesis.verdict: CALIBRATED. #114's one number (56.08% per-token argmax divergence) resolves into the #192 per-reading menu: (A) STRICT per-sequence token-identity pass = 0.1250 (16/128 sequences zero-flip) -> the int4-spec stack FAILS literal token-identity for 87.5% of sequences; (B) per-token-theta is the CDF menu (theta=0 -> 0.1250, theta=1 -> 1.0); (C) PPL-ONLY (the auto-scorer's actual check) = 100% (served PPL 2.3772 <= 2.42). HONEST CORRECTION to the naive iid intuition: independence predicts strict-A=(1-p)^512=10^-183~0, but the EMPIRICAL strict-A is 0.1250 because the #114 flips CASCADE (extreme within-sequence clustering: 16 prompts never trip, 112 trip-and-cascade). The model-free Frechet bound caps strict-A at 1-p=0.4392 for ANY correlation structure, so even MAXIMAL clustering keeps strict-A < 0.5 << 1 (strict_a_robust_to_clustering=True). BOTH the 481.53 frontier and the land #71 tree ride the SAME int4-spec basis, so the menu applies to both. The RULING is the human's (issue #192); only the wirbel #199/#213/#216 compliant-kernel route survives strict-A. BASELINE 481.53 untouched. NOT a launch.
